title: Make new endpoint /lang — GET /lang?lang=<code> returns localised welcome message as JSON
description:
## Overview

Extend the `GET /lang` endpoint (added in sub-task 01) to accept an optional `lang` query parameter. When provided with a valid language code, the response must include a localised welcome message for that language. When the parameter is absent or invalid, fall back to English (`en`).

The `TRANSLATIONS` constant in `app.rb` already contains per-language keys; a `welcome` key (or equivalent greeting key) should be used — or added to `TRANSLATIONS` if one does not yet exist.

## Reference

Parent ticket: https://linear.app/axbots/issue/TES-25/make-new-endpoint-lang

## Expected response (e.g. GET /lang?lang=pl)

```json
{
  "languages": ["en", "pl", "de", "fr"],
  "selected_language": "pl",
  "welcome": "Witaj"
}
```

## Fallback behaviour (unknown lang or no lang param)

```json
{
  "languages": ["en", "pl", "de", "fr"],
  "selected_language": "en",
  "welcome": "Welcome"
}
```

## Implementation notes

- Reuse the same `GET /lang` route from sub-task 01; this sub-task extends its behaviour
- Use `TRANSLATIONS.key?(request.params['lang'])` for validation (same pattern as `/about`)
- Each language entry in `TRANSLATIONS` must have a `welcome` key with an appropriate greeting string
- Response `content-type` must remain `application/json`
scope: Extend GET /lang to accept a lang query param and return a localised welcome message in the JSON response.
acceptance_criteria: GET /lang?lang=en returns welcome message in English
GET /lang?lang=pl returns welcome message in Polish
GET /lang?lang=de returns welcome message in German
GET /lang?lang=fr returns welcome message in French
GET /lang with no lang param defaults to 'en'
RSpec tests cover valid language selection, default fallback, and unknown language fallback
relation_to_original: Implements the 'print welcome with selected language' bullet point from the parent ticket.
